chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-225 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case(Priority) 1.For orders on o/objections 2.For hearing of CMA 1229/23 3.For hearing of main case 15-05-2024 Mr. Mujahid Hussain Phulpoto, Advocate for petitioners. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant A.G a/w Arbab Ali Solangi, Director, Fisheries Sukkur, Ghulam Yaseen, Deputy Director, Fisheries, Khairpur, SIP Mushtaque Ahmed, SHO, P.S, Tando Masti Khan and SIP Jameel Ahmed on behalf of SSP, Khairpur. ***** Petitioners claim to be license-holders, issued by the Fisheries Department, having right to fish in lakes situated in Block S. No.200, Deh Wassan, Drib Mehar Shah, Taluka and district Khairpur. Their case is that the government officials/respondents at the instance of private respondents No.10&11 are trying to hinder them fishing in the subject lakes and consequently dispossess them. The officials from the Fisheries Department, present, submit that some of the petitioners’ licenses expired in 2023, whereas, licenses of the others have been extended till 30th June, 2024. They undertake that they would examine the record properly to find out if license of any of the petitioners is still running and renewed or not. In case, they find petitioners have the licenses/cards to fish in the subject lake till 30th June, 2024, they will not interfere in their fishing rights. The police officials present in Court also submit that they would act in accordance with law on the directions of Director, Fisheries Sukkur on the subject. 2 Learned AAG submits that no prejudice would be caused to the petitioners and their grievance would be resolved in accordance with law.

In case, they find petitioners have the licenses/cards to fish in the subject lake till 30th June, 2024, they will not interfere in their fishing rights. The police officials present in Court also submit that they would act in accordance with law on the directions of Director, Fisheries Sukkur on the subject. 2 Learned AAG submits that no prejudice would be caused to the petitioners and their grievance would be resolved in accordance with law. This petitioners’ counsel is satisfied and the petition along with listed application, in view of above, is accordingly disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
